summary: cacheKey() inserts '&' between sorted param entries so different param sets cannot collide on a single cache file.
reason: Greptile review (PR #576): the original loop concatenated 'k=v' with no separator, so {"a":"1b","c":"2"} and {"a":"1","bc":"2"} both produced 'a=1bc=2' — different queries could silently share a cached response.
validated_outcome: go build, go vet, go test ./internal/pricebook/ all clean
